title: crepis commutata
intro: Crepis commutata, also known as rosin rose or rosin weed, is a flowering plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to its sesquiterpene lactone content. Crepis commutata has been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is sometimes used in herbal remedies to support overall health and well-being.
